explanation: The requirement states that in PSV Mode, a new inspiration is initiated when there is a sudden drop in airway pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity threshold (ITS_PSV), which is set by the user. There is an atomic proposition 'PSVMode' for being in the right mode and 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV' for detecting a pressure drop above the threshold. To capture the described behavior, the property should specify that whenever the controller detects both that it is in PSVMode and there is a pressure drop above ITS_PSV, a new inspiration (inspiratory phase) must be started immediately. Hence, the structured NL follows the pattern: 'whenever [PSVMode & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV], immediately satisfy inspiratoryPhaseStart'.
decision1_substring: A new inspiration shall be initiated by a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (ITS_PSV ), which shall be set by the user (i.e., it yields the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV ))
decision3_substring: A new inspiration shall be initiated by a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (ITS_PSV ), which shall be set by the user (i.e., it yields the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV ))
output_structured_natural_language: whenever PSVMode & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV, immediately satisfy inspiratoryPhaseStart

explanation: The requirement specifies the behavior of the ventilator in PSV Mode: a new breath (inspiration) should start when there is a sudden drop in pressure above the ITS_PSV threshold. The atomic proposition 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV' directly indicates that such a drop has been detected in the PSV mode. Thus, the structured NL property should express that whenever the system is in PSVMode and this drop condition occurs, a new inspiration must be initiated (this could be captured by starting the inspiratory phase, i.e., 'inspiratoryPhaseStart'). The most direct logical translation is: Whenever (PSVMode & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PSV), the system must eventually satisfy inspiratoryPhaseStart (start a new inspiration).
